title: Oxalis leonis
intro: Oxalis leonis is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae), characterized by its small, delicate flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Oxalis leonis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Oxalis leonis is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Oxalis, which includes over 800 species of plants.
